WFH due to Covid. I guess most of you are also WFH now. However, WFH even drives people crazy because workload would be even heavier. You would forget that lunch time should be within 1230-1400, and 1800 should be the time to get off work.

I have joined this recruitment team for 2 years. I have called and seen various candidates. Some are very friendly and nice to chat with. (We could even become friends!!!) Some would ghost forever, and you would never be able to reach them. Some are very rude and insane…

Today I would like to share the worst and the rudest candidate I have ever met. He has made me so mad. (P.S. He is from LinkedIn and working in HK). Please feel free to share your similar experience with me. It might help us deal with these candidates. You may either comment below or message me.

我會咁寫

Due to COVID 19, I believe most of you are working from home as I am. I am positive that most of you could agree with me that the new working style is sometime a little frustrating. When WFH, it is easy to lost track of time. Lunch hour and time to get off become blurry and less distinct.

I have been in this recruitment team for 2 years and I have worked with candidates from all spectrums. Some candidates are friendly and are delights to talk to (and becomes friends with). Some are not as easy going and would have kept straightly professional.

In this post, I would like to share with you one of the most difficult candidate in the scope of my career. I have to admit that I wasn’t please with our acquaintances (P.s. He reached out to me on LinkedIn and he was working in HK). I wasn’t sure if my response to the situation is preferable. I am, therefore, open for discussion. Please feel free to leave your thoughts and experiences in the comment. I hope this post could turn into a tread that is beneficial to all of us who might have been dealing with similar situations. Do not hesitate to comment down below or reach out to me via private message.

我會咁寫

Due to COVID 19, I believe most of you are working from home as I am. I am positive that most of you could agree with me that the new working style is sometime a little frustrating. When WFH, it is easy to lost track of time. Lunch hour and time to get off become blurry and less distinct.

I have been in this recruitment team for 2 years and I have worked with candidates from all spectrums. Some candidates are friendly and are delights to talk to (and becomes friends with). Some are not as easy going and would have kept straightly professional.

In this post, I would like to share with you one of the most difficult candidate in the scope of my career. I have to admit that I wasn’t please with our acquaintances (P.s. He reached out to me on LinkedIn and he was working in HK). I wasn’t sure if my response to the situation is preferable. I am, therefore, open for discussion. Please feel free to leave your thoughts and experiences in the comment. I hope this post could turn into a tread that is beneficial to all of us who might have been dealing with similar situations. Do not hesitate to comment down below or reach out to me via private message.

WFH due to Covid. I guess most of you are also WFH now. However, WFH even drives people crazy because workload would be even heavier. You would forget that lunch time should be within 1230-1400, and 1800 should be the time to get off work.

I have joined this recruitment team for 2 years. I have called and seen various candidates. Some are very friendly and nice to chat with. (We could even become friends!!!) Some would ghost forever, and you would never be able to reach them. Some are very rude and insane…

Today I would like to share the worst and the rudest candidate I have ever met. He has made me so mad. (P.S. He is from LinkedIn and working in HK). Please feel free to share your similar experience with me. It might help us deal with these candidates. You may either comment below or message me.

我會咁寫

Due to COVID 19, I believe most of you are working from home as I am. I am positive that most of you could agree with me that the new working style is sometime a little frustrating. When WFH, it is easy to lost track of time. Lunch hour and time to get off become blurry and less distinct.

I have been in this recruitment team for 2 years and I have worked with candidates from all spectrums. Some candidates are friendly and are delights to talk to (and becomes friends with). Some are not as easy going and would have kept straightly professional.

In this post, I would like to share with you one of the most difficult candidate in the scope of my career. I have to admit that I wasn’t please with our acquaintances (P.s. He reached out to me on LinkedIn and he was working in HK). I wasn’t sure if my response to the situation is preferable. I am, therefore, open for discussion. Please feel free to leave your thoughts and experiences in the comment. I hope this post could turn into a tread that is beneficial to all of us who might have been dealing with similar situations. Do not hesitate to comment down below or reach out to me via private message.

btw, spectrum可以咁樣用？定係想講all walks of life?

Pleased with好似通常係oneself? 可能我見識少，但通常見都係self-satisfied咁
